title: FEMS bitemporal indexing: valid_from/valid_until + recorded_at/invalidated_at on MemoryItem (folded WP-1-FEMS-Bitemporal-Indexing)
goal: Add bi-temporal timestamps to FEMS V1 MemoryItem so MemoryCapsule replay can reconstruct exactly which memories existed AND were considered valid at a given instant. Ports the Graphiti/Zep temporal knowledge graph pattern. Folded from WP-1-FEMS-Bitemporal-Indexing into KERNEL-004 cluster D.3.
expected_diff_shape: Implements the existing pure-Rust bitemporal primitive in src/backend/handshake_core/src/memory/bitemporal.rs and adds Postgres-backed persistence through kernel_event_ledger JSONB payload events. No memory_item table is created. Migration 0029_bitemporal_event_ledger_indexes.sql adds expression indexes over the item payload's valid_from, valid_until, recorded_at, and invalidated_at JSON paths plus a manifest replay index. Tests live in src/backend/handshake_core/tests/bitemporal_indexing_tests.rs and Cargo.toml registers the test target.
unblock_conditions: MT-141 (MemoryCapsule), MT-143 (CapsuleBuilder). FEMS V1 surfaces in main.
implementation_notes: Per WP-1-FEMS-Bitemporal-Indexing preserved_intent, MT-157 preserves the two-axis model: valid_from/valid_until model when the fact is true in the world, and recorded_at/invalidated_at model when the system recorded or invalidated it. The original memory_item table migration shape was invalid for the current product because memory persistence is kernel_event_ledger. The implemented path is option (b): store bitemporal item events in kernel_event_ledger JSONB payloads under aggregate_type `memory_bitemporal_item`, maintain a manifest aggregate for replay enumeration, and add JSON-path expression indexes for both temporal axes. The Postgres adapter rejects invalid windows before append, deduplicates idempotent records through ledger idempotency keys, atomically appends item and manifest events, schema-filters raw payloads before full decode, and folds item events by effective recorded time for true as-of replay.
risk_if_missed: Without bi-temporal indexing, replay cannot distinguish 'memory existed but was superseded' from 'memory didn't exist yet'; outcome feedback (MT-158) cannot attribute outcomes to the exact pack composition.
validator_focus: Both temporal axes (world time + recorded time) honored independently. Migration is non-destructive. Indexes cover the common query shape.

fixes.summary: Bitemporal isolated-Postgres helper applies only event-ledger and bitemporal-index migrations, then constructs PostgresDatabase from that pool.
fixes.gap_closed: Removed slow full-project migration replay from every bitemporal test while preserving the actual table/index assertions under live Postgres.

tests.command: POSTGRES_TEST_URL=postgres://postgres:***@127.0.0.1:65432/handshake_test?sslmode=disable CARGO_TARGET_DIR=D:/Projects/LLM projects/Handshake/Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target/wp-kernel-004-postgres-bitemporal cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test bitemporal_indexing_tests -- --ignored --test-threads=1 --nocapture
tests.note: Initial run exceeded timeout applying full migrations per test. Helper now applies only 0018_kernel_event_ledger and 0029_bitemporal_event_ledger_indexes into isolated schemas; final suite green.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.

blocker.resolution: Selected option (b): wire bitemporal stamps into kernel_event_ledger JSONB payload events and add typed JSON-path expression indexes. No memory_item table was created.
blocker.summary: MT-157 contract owned_files names src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/2026_05_18_fems_bitemporal.sql which would add bitemporal columns (valid_from, valid_until, recorded_at, invalidated_at) to a memory_item Postgres table. NO SUCH TABLE EXISTS in the current schema. The actual MemoryItem persistence substrate is the kernel_event_ledger table (per src/backend/handshake_core/src/memory/persistence_postgres.rs MT-145 + MT-146 docstring: 'The durable persistence surface is the kernel_event_ledger Postgres table, accessed through Database::append_kernel_event'). The bitemporal.rs pure-Rust primitive (BitemporalStamps + AsOfQuery + BitemporalIndex, 344 lines, 9 inline tests) is correctly implemented and operates against any Vec<MemoryItem>; the gap is that there is no Postgres-side wiring of those bitemporal stamps into the actual event-ledger payload column. Operator decision needed: (a) create a dedicated memory_item Postgres table backed by the bitemporal columns, OR (b) wire bitemporal stamps into kernel_event_ledger JSON payload columns and add typed JSON-path indexes, OR (c) rescope MT-157 to ship only the pure-Rust primitive and defer the Postgres migration to a downstream MT.
evidence: ls src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/ | grep -i bitemporal -> NO MATCH
grep -l 'BitemporalStamps' src/backend/handshake_core/src/memory/ -r -> only bitemporal.rs (no persistence_postgres.rs reference)
src/memory/persistence_postgres.rs documents kernel_event_ledger as the authority surface for memory capsule persistence, not a memory_item table
blocker.operator_action_required: NONE. Resolved by implementing option (b), the existing product-authority kernel_event_ledger path.
blocker.blocking_record: Recorded in this MT-157.lifecycle.blocker; sibling defect class to MT-176/MT-190 PATH_SHAPE_DRIFT operator-decision items.
blocker.pure_rust_primitive_status: READY_FOR_VALIDATION at the bitemporal.rs primitive level — 9 inline #[cfg(test)] tests cover visible_at world+system axis filtering, invalidation, query-equivalence replay.

test_logs.outcome: RED regression: later event_sequence incorrectly masked earlier as-of state before fix.
PASS: 5 ignored live Postgres tests passed with POSTGRES_TEST_URL set.
PASS: 13 FEMS V1 integration tests passed.

subagent_evidence.summary: Selected field-aligned option (b): kernel_event_ledger JSONB payloads and expression indexes, avoiding a non-existent memory_item table.
Confirmed bitemporal primitive exists, memory_item table does not, and kernel_event_ledger is the actual persistence substrate.
Added JSONB expression index migration and down migration only.
Initial findings covered as-of replay, atomic appends, and schema pre-filtering; all were fixed before RFV.
PASS: focused MT-157 re-review found no blocking findings and verified prior findings 1-3 were addressed. Live Postgres bitemporal target passed 5/5 during re-review.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Nonexistent memory_item table would create schema drift.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: No memory_item table or columns were created; persistence uses kernel_event_ledger JSONB events.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Later ledger event_sequence could mask earlier as-of state.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Postgres replay folds item events by effective recorded time and event_sequence tie-breaker.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Crash between item append and manifest append could strand an item event.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Item and manifest events append through append_kernel_event_pair_atomic_with_causation.

risks_and_mitigations.risk: Malformed or future-schema payloads could break replay.
risks_and_mitigations.mitigation: Raw payload schema_id is checked before full typed deserialization.

residual_risks: Downstream MT-163 may choose to reuse PostgresBitemporalMemoryIndex or wrap it in a higher-level accessor; no MT-157 acceptance criterion requires that wrapper.

minimum_controls: Migration is non-destructive; existing rows backfilled with sane defaults.
Indexes added for the common (valid_from, valid_until) and (recorded_at, invalidated_at) query patterns.
AsOfQuery exposes both axes; callers cannot accidentally collapse to single-temporal queries.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: Bitemporal axes are independently honored, invalidation is non-destructive, Postgres adapter uses atomic pair-append for item+manifest events with idempotent ledger keys, and as-of replay correctly folds events by effective recorded time with event_sequence tie-breaker.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: Bitemporal axes are independently honored, invalidation is non-destructive, Postgres adapter uses atomic pair-append for item+manifest events with idempotent ledger keys, and as-of replay correctly folds events by effective recorded time with event_sequence tie-breaker.
